source: International Association of Genocide Scholars / Taner Akçam
note: Turkey disputes genocide classification and estimates; widely recognized internationally. Ottoman archives partially support higher estimates.

source: Australian War Memorial / Turkish General Staff
note: Allied dead: British 43,000, ANZAC 11,500, French 10,000. Ottoman figures less precisely documented.
Civilian death totals include Greeks, Armenians, and Muslims killed during population displacements; very difficult to separate from earlier WWI violence.

killed: 3 (PM Menderes, FM Zorlu, Finance Min. Polatkan)
note: Celal Bayar's death sentence was commuted. 15 others received death sentences, also commuted. Menderes and Zorlu hanged September 17, 1961 on İmralı Island.

killed: ~3,000 killed (Greek Cypriot and Turkish Cypriot, both sides)
note: 1,510 Greek Cypriots listed as missing; Turkish Cypriot missing persons also unaccounted. Ongoing Committee on Missing Persons in Cyprus.
Breakdown disputed: ~7,000 Turkish military/police; ~17,000 PKK fighters; ~5,000+ civilians. Kurdish sources allege higher civilian deaths from state operations. 3,000+ villages evacuated.
Deaths include coup participants, security forces, and civilians. Parliament building, presidential palace bombed. 150,000+ subsequently detained or dismissed in purges.
Mw 7.8 + Mw 7.5 on same day. 125,000+ buildings damaged or destroyed. 1.5 million displaced. Deadliest earthquake in Turkey since 1939 Erzincan quake (33,000 dead).
Deaths included 1 police officer. 4,900+ detained; 11 lost eyes from tear gas or rubber bullets; 5 in coma at peak. Protests spread to 79 cities.

source: Hew Strachan, The First World War / demographic estimates
note: Includes ~771,000 military dead, plus massive civilian mortality from genocide, famine, disease, and deportations. Population of Anatolia fell by ~20% during WWI era.
